SYNTHETIC NOTE. Not a real patient. For testing only.

Patient: Jane Testcase (synthetic), DOB 1975-01-01 (synthetic)
Date of visit: 2026-06-01

Chief complaint: Low back pain radiating to left leg, 8 weeks duration.

History of present illness: Patient reports gradual onset low back pain
with radiation into the left posterior thigh and calf. Pain is worse with
prolonged sitting and improves partially with rest. Denies saddle
anesthesia, bowel or bladder dysfunction, or fever.

Conservative treatment to date: Completed 6 weeks of physical therapy with
minimal improvement. Trial of NSAIDs (naproxen) with partial relief. No
prior epidural steroid injection.

Physical exam: Positive straight leg raise on the left at 40 degrees.
Diminished sensation in the left L5 dermatome. Reflexes symmetric.
Strength 4/5 left extensor hallucis longus.

Assessment: Lumbar radiculopathy, left L5 distribution, persistent despite
6 weeks of conservative therapy.

Plan: Requesting lumbar MRI without contrast to evaluate for disc
herniation or nerve root compression given failed conservative therapy.
